result:
## Summary

Created the X/Twitter DM channel extension at `extensions/x-twitter/` with **12 files** and **25 passing tests**, zero type errors.

### Files created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `extensions/x-twitter/package.json` | Package config with `@openclaw/x-twitter` name, channel metadata |
| `extensions/x-twitter/openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest (kind=channels) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/index.ts` | Plugin entry point, registers channel via `api.registerChannel()` |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/runtime.ts` | Plugin runtime getter/setter |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/types.ts` | TypeScript types (`CoreConfig`, `XTwitterProbe`, `ResolvedXTwitterAccount`, etc.) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/accounts.ts` | Account resolution, multi-account support, env var fallbacks (`X_API_KEY`, etc.) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/normalize.ts` | Username/ID normalization, `@` stripping, validation |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/x-api.ts` | X API v2 client with OAuth 1.0a HMAC-SHA256 signing. Endpoints: `/users/me`, `/dm_conversations/.../messages`, `/dm_events`, `/users/by/username/...` |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/probe.ts` | Health check via `GET /2/users/me` to verify credentials |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/send.ts` | Outbound DM delivery with username-to-ID resolution |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/monitor.ts` | Inbound DM polling loop with configurable interval, self-message filtering, seen-set capping |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/onboarding.ts` | CLI wizard for API credentials, env var detection, DM policy, allowlist |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/channel.ts` | Full `ChannelPlugin` implementation with all adapters (config, pairing, security, messaging, resolver, directory, outbound, status, gateway) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/channel.test.ts` | **25 unit tests** covering normalize, accounts, plugin adapters, and onboarding |

### Key design decisions

- **OAuth 1.0a** with HMAC-SHA256 signing (required by X API v2 DM endpoints for user-context auth)
- **Polling-based** inbound (X API v2 doesn't offer webhooks for free-tier DMs; configurable interval, default 15s)
- **DM-only** capabilities (no group support, matching X DM model)
- **Multi-account** support following the standard pattern
- **Env var fallbacks**: `X_API_KEY`, `X_API_KEY_SECRET`, `X_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `X_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET`
